tools.name: search_events
tools.description: Search for upcoming tabletop gaming events (tournaments, leagues, casual play) near a location.

tools.name: search_venues
tools.description: Search for local game stores, hobby centers, and community spaces.

tools.name: search_games
tools.description: Search for board games in our catalog to find their BoardGameGeek IDs (required for filtering events by game).

tools.name: rsvp_event
tools.description: RSVP / join a TavernFinder event on behalf of the authenticated user. Requires a personal access token with the `write:rsvp` scope. Use search_events first to find the venueId + eventId pair.

tools.name: register_user
tools.description: Create a new TavernFinder account. Returns access + refresh tokens that the agent can use immediately. The new user is unverified until they click the email verification link, but most read paths still work.

tools.name: create_event
tools.description: Create a new event (open event or private table) at a venue on behalf of the user. Requires a personal access token with the `write:create_event` scope. The user must be a member of the venue (player role at minimum); only staff/CL can create open events.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: register_user · Private data: rsvp_event, register_user, create_event · Public sink: register_user.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: register_user · Private data: rsvp_event, register_user, create_event · Public sink: register_user

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
